epigraph.text: For just as each of us has one body with many members, and these members do not all have the same function, so in Christ we, though many, form one body.

content.text:
Once we prove triangles congruent, we unlock information about all 
their corresponding parts. This principle is called CPCTC.
CPCTC: Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are Congruent

content.title: CPCTC
content.content:
**Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are Congruent.**

If $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF$, then:

- All corresponding sides are congruent

- All corresponding angles are congruent
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
CPCTC is used *after* proving triangles congruent to conclude 
that specific parts are congruent.

content.title: CPCTC proof
content.problem:
Given: $M$ is the midpoint of $\overline{AB}$ and $\overline{CD}$

Prove: $\overline{AC} \cong \overline{BD}$
content.solution:
proof

1. $M$ is the midpoint of $\overline{AB}$ and $\overline{CD}$ 
 (Given)

2. $\overline{AM} \cong \overline{BM}$ (Definition of midpoint)

3. $\overline{CM} \cong \overline{DM}$ (Definition of midpoint)

4. $\angle AMC \cong \angle BMD$ (Vertical angles are congruent)

5. $\triangle AMC \cong \triangle BMD$ (SAS Postulate)

6. $\overline{AC} \cong \overline{BD}$ (**CPCTC**)
proof

content.title: Planning a CPCTC Proof
content.content:
1. Identify what you need to prove (a side or angle congruence)

2. Find triangles that contain those parts as corresponding parts

3. Prove those triangles congruent using SSS, SAS, ASA, or AAS

4. Apply CPCTC to conclude the desired congruence

content.title: Proving angles congruent
content.solution:
Plan: $\angle A$ is in $\triangle ABD$ and $\angle C$ is in 
$\triangle CBD$. Prove these triangles congruent.

proof

1. $\overline{AB} \cong \overline{CB}$ (Given)

2. $\overline{AD} \cong \overline{CD}$ (Given)

3. $\overline{BD} \cong \overline{BD}$ (Reflexive Property)

4. $\triangle ABD \cong \triangle CBD$ (SSS Postulate)

5. $\angle A \cong \angle C$ (CPCTC)
proof

items: Lines are parallel (via congruent alternate interior angles)

marginNotes.text:
For just as each of us has one body 
with many members, and these members do not all have the same function, 
so in Christ we, though many, form one body.

The plans of the diligent lead to 
profit as surely as haste leads to poverty.

marginNotes.author: Bertrand Russell
marginNotes.text:
Mathematics, rightly viewed, possesses 
not only truth, but supreme beauty.
